Police checked the vehicle's license plate and discovered it was reported stolen out of Gurney, Illinois.
An immediate attempt to locate alert was issued for other squad cars to search the area.
Upon contacting a neighboring police agency, it was confirmed that they were already aware of the stolen vehicle, which had been involved in recent pursuits in Illinois.
It is also believed that the driver was 17-year-old Herman Thomas, who was accompanied by 18-year-old Jamari Randall, 18-year-old Mark Williams, and another 17-year-old.
These individuals were identified as gang members and were known to carry firearms.
Additionally, Herman had posted a live video of themselves in the Jaguar on the day they had stolen it.
At one point, the teen sped past undercover detectives.
Other officers attempted to locate the vehicle, but repeatedly lost track of it in various parts of the city.
Eventually, they were located again and officers pursued.
